libraryPreparationPreflight.agent_guidance: Use an application context based test rather than only instantiating POJOs. Register a SimpleMeterRegistry bean to reach the meter observation handler. To exercise the tracer-present branch, provide a minimal or mocked Tracer bean. Set Spring properties such as spring.ai.chat.observations.log-prompt=true, spring.ai.chat.observations.log-completion=true, and spring.ai.chat.observations.include-error-logging=true when asserting the conditional logging/error handler beans.

deterministic_setup.reason: Provides the ChatModel type and Spring AI chat/client/model observation handlers and contexts referenced by ChatObservationAutoConfiguration.
Provides the Spring Boot auto-configuration annotations, conditions, and configuration properties infrastructure used by the library.
Provides the Tracer API needed to load and exercise the tracer-present auto-configuration branch.
Provides ApplicationContextRunner-style test support appropriate for exercising Spring Boot auto-configuration.

risks: The requested version is an RC built against Spring Boot 4.1.0-RC1 and Micrometer 1.7.0-RC1, so dependency alignment matters.
A Tracer bean is only needed for the tracer-present branches; tests that merely verify the non-tracing branch can omit it but will leave less meaningful coverage.
libraryPreparationPreflight.summary: The artifact is a Spring Boot/Spring AI auto-configuration module whose POM marks its class-bearing collaborators as optional; resolving the requested coordinate alone brings in no transitive dependencies, and jdeps shows the auto-configuration classes reference missing Spring AI chat/model observation, Spring Boot autoconfigure, and Micrometer tracing types. Meaningful coverage needs those optional runtime APIs plus Boot test support for an application-context based test.
